one_time_description: Loss on debt forgiveness recorded in Nation Support segment
economic_notes: Nation purchased a resort for $785,000 subsequent to year end. Established Bendziny Tourism Ventures Limited Partnership and Bendziny Tourism Ltd. to operate the resort. Tsi Del Del Timber Development Limited Partnership (99.99% owned) generated net income of $493,538. COVID-19 related funding of $938,071 received ($894,339 from ISC, $43,732 from FNHA). Redstone Gas Bar operations consolidated. Forest range revenue generated.
notes: Fiscal year is March 31, 2021 not 2024. Seven entities fully consolidated including Tsi'deldel Development Corporation, Tsi'deldel Limited Partnership, Crazy Horse Energy Drink Ltd., Crazy Horse Energy Drink Limited Partnership, Redstone Gas Bar Ltd., Redstone Gas Bar Limited Partnership, and Ts'iDel Del Resources Ltd. One GBE (Tsi Del Del Timber Development Limited Partnership at 99.99%) accounted for by modified equity method. Five entities recorded at cost (Tsi Del Del Enterprises Ltd., River West Forest Products Ltd., Eniyud Community Forests Ltd., Cariboo Indian Enterprises Ltd., Central Chilcotin Rehabilitation Ltd.). Restricted cash of $880,639 includes social housing ($43,970) and gaming ($836,669). Ottawa Trust Funds of $1,058,736 included in accumulated surplus. Subsequent event: resort purchased for $785,000 after year end. No schedule of remuneration provided in the extracted text. Infrastructure assets of $1,596,596 are under construction with no amortization recorded. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($5,197,362) + FNHA ($4,216,338) + CMHC ($52,460) + Tsilhqot'in National Government ($86,857) - however TNG may not be a government transfer in traditional sense; OSR calculated as total revenue minus ISC, FNHA, CMHC and TNG transfers.
